reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_97]]
Speaker 3: How are these programs designed to align with the most cutting-edge industry trends and societal needs in the United States?

[[BLOCK:srt_98]]
Speaker 1: Why don’t you take this one?

[[BLOCK:srt_99]]
Speaker 1: Sure, no problem.

[[BLOCK:srt_100]]
Speaker 1: For example, we have an outstanding psychology (psychology) program. It’s one of our fastest-growing, most popular, and most distinctive offerings.

[[BLOCK:srt_101]]
Speaker 1: We’ve introduced this well-established program to China, integrating San Francisco’s professional strengths with the expertise of leading practitioners and professors in China who are at the forefront of the psychology field.

[[BLOCK:srt_102]]
Speaker 1: In essence, it’s a fusion of two cultures and two systems of thought.

[[BLOCK:srt_103]]
Speaker 1: I believe that fusion has made it an exceptional program. As for artificial intelligence, we are located at the very heart of San Francisco’s artificial intelligence revolution.

[[BLOCK:srt_104]]
Speaker 1: You could say we are deeply intertwined with the city’s growth. We’re surrounded by companies, and the surge of investment in San Francisco is happening right around us—virtually encircling Golden Gate University.

[[BLOCK:srt_105]]
Speaker 1: That places us squarely at the center of the artificial intelligence wave, which we see as a key focus of the university’s future development.

[[BLOCK:srt_106]]
Speaker 1: That’s one of the main reasons we’re so excited about launching our new degree program—the Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence (Masters of Science in Artificial Intelligence).

[[BLOCK:srt_107]]
Speaker 1: We’re pleased to offer this degree in multiple formats, including online courses in English, online courses in Chinese, and in-person classes at our San Francisco campus.

[[BLOCK:srt_108]]
Speaker 1: So whether—

[[BLOCK:srt_109]]
Speaker 2: I don’t have much to add, except to say that many of our alumni are already active in these fields and working in related industries.
